title: Fix atom interpolation for dynamic role atoms
prompt:
The following function builds atoms by interpolating a user-controlled role string:

```elixir
defmodule MyApp.Roles do
  def role_to_atom(role_str) when is_binary(role_str) do
    :"role_#{role_str}"
  end
end
```

This is an atom-exhaustion DoS — each unique `role_str` permanently grows the atom table. Rewrite the module to map known role strings to a fixed set of atoms without ever calling `String.to_atom/1` or using the `:"..."` sigil with an interpolated value. Default unknown roles to `:user`. Write the fixed code to `lib/my_app/roles.ex`.
tier_rationale: Tests the non-obvious atom-interpolation sigil variant. Fix is a simple case/cond block.
